2605.28616.title: Measuring Form and Function in Language Models
2605.28616.paper_summary: We introduce quantitative metrics for child language acquisition to evaluate language models. Our focus is on the formal syntactic and functional discourse properties of determiners in English, which young children acquire early and accurately. We propose Contextual Alternative Choice (CAC), a new prompting method which provides targeted tests for both syntactic and discourse knowledge of language. The method enables direct comparison of language models against children, and more importantly, against statistical benchmarks independently established in empirical research. No current model trained on a comparable amount of data simultaneously meet both formal and functional benchmarks like human children, but some very large models do. We present our results as methodological and technical contributions, with specific emphasis on cognitive status of language models.
2605.28616.paper_summary_zh: 我們引入量化指標來評估語言模型的兒童語言習得。我們的重點是英語中限定詞的正式句法和功能性話語特徵，這些特徵是年幼的孩子早期且準確地習得的。我們提出了上下文替代選擇（CAC），這是一種新的提示方法，提供針對語言的句法和話語知識的有針對性的測試。該方法使語言模型能夠與兒童進行直接比較，更重要的是，與在實證研究中獨立建立的統計基準進行比較。目前沒有任何模型在相似數量的數據上訓練，能同時滿足正式和功能基準，像人類兒童一樣，但一些非常大的模型可以做到。我們將結果作為方法論和技術貢獻呈現，特別強調語言模型的認知狀態。
2605.28616.author: Héctor Javier Vázquez Martínez et.al.
2605.28616.authors: Héctor Javier Vázquez Martínez, Charles Yang
